description: Single source of truth for stacks, versions, and module identifiers exposed by the interactive installer (`scripts/install.sh`) and the `/dhpk:setup` slash command. Adding a new module means: (1) add an entry here, (2) create modules/<id>/module.yaml + skills/, (3) register the skills path in .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Optional stack field `selection` defaults to `exclusive` (single-version pick); set to `additive` for stacks where library projects need to enable multiple versions cumulatively (e.g. a package spanning PHP 7.4 → 8.x). Optional per-version field `exclusive: true` (only meaningful under `additive`) marks a version that cannot combine with siblings in the same stack — the installer drops the others when an exclusive pick is selected.

stacks.name: PHP
stacks.description: PHP language baseline. Pick 5.6 for legacy monoliths (forbids 7.0+ syntax, polyfill guidance) — note 5.6 is marked exclusive and cannot combine with later versions. Pick 7.4 for the modern baseline (ships post-edit php-cs-fixer hook + pre-commit lint+phpstan+psalm gate). Pick 8.x additively on top of 7.4 for projects whose floor is ≥8.0, or for library packages that span 7.x→8.x and want guidance on conditionally-usable 8.x features (match, nullsafe, named args, attributes, readonly, enums, intersection types). Selection is additive — pick multiple versions to stack cumulative guidance.

stacks.name: Laravel Framework
stacks.description: Laravel framework — per-version modules for each major from 6 through 11. Library packages spanning multiple Laravel majors should enable all the versions in their composer constraint to get cumulative version-specific guidance (deprecations, signature additions, BC traps). Applications running a single Laravel major should pick only that version. Pair with skills/laravel-package-author/SKILL.md (cross-cutting, framework-agnostic).

stacks.name: Yii Framework
stacks.description: Yii PHP framework. The 1.1 module covers autoload, ActiveRecord / CDbCriteria, DDD layering, XSS/CSRF defaults.

stacks.name: PHPUnit
stacks.description: PHPUnit testing framework. Pick 5.7 for legacy PHP 5.6 / pre-PHPUnit-8 stacks. Pick 9 for PHP 7.3+ projects (latest version compatible with that PHP floor). Pick 10 for PHP 8.1+ projects (attribute-style annotations preferred). Pick 11 for PHP 8.2+ projects (doc-comment annotations fully removed; attributes-only). Selection is additive — a project mid-migration can enable two adjacent versions to consult both sets of guidance. The phpunit-10 / phpunit-11 modules document attribute migration paths; project's composer.json PHP constraint must independently satisfy each module's runtime requirement.

stacks.name: JavaScript / TypeScript
stacks.description: ESLint 9 flat config tier strategy, per-leaf `// @ts-check` progressive typing, post-edit ESLint feedback (async), pre-commit lint + typecheck gate. Framework-agnostic; works for legacy monolith bundles and modern bundler/SPA projects alike.

stacks.name: Swift
stacks.description: Swift 6 language baseline with Swift 5.10 / iOS 17 compatibility notes AND Swift 6.2+ (Xcode 26+) approachable-concurrency defaults (async stays on the caller, @concurrent opt-in offload, isolated conformances, MainActor default inference). Strict concurrency (Sendable/actors, async-await), optionals discipline, value-vs-reference, error handling. The foundation module the rest of the iOS suite (swiftui / ios-platform / swift-testing / xcode-tooling) requires.

stacks.name: SwiftUI
stacks.description: SwiftUI app architecture: MVVM + Coordinator, Observation framework (@Observable / @Bindable), NavigationStack + type-safe routing, state-ownership rules, Combine + UIKit interop.

stacks.name: iOS Platform SDK
stacks.description: iOS SDK frameworks for a health/PHI app: Core Data at-rest encryption (SQLCipher / File Protection), CryptoKit + Keychain, an actor-based offline local-store pattern (in-memory cache + atomic file write), Vision OCR (with handwriting limits + manual fallback), AVFoundation, LocalAuthentication, UserNotifications (Time Sensitive / 64-pending / rollover), HealthKit read-only, privacy-manifest / no-iCloud compliance.

stacks.name: Swift Testing
stacks.description: iOS test strategy across XCTest and Swift Testing (@Test / #expect / #require / parameterized), XCUITest, and swift-snapshot-testing, with a 3-layer unit/integration/UI taxonomy plus protocol-based DI for host-testable (`swift test`) file/Keychain/network code.

stacks.name: Xcode Tooling
stacks.description: SwiftLint config strategy + post-edit SwiftLint hook (async, self-skip) and a pre-commit xcodebuild/SPM build+test gate (self-skip when toolchain absent; build uses a device-name-free generic destination and tests auto-fall back to an available simulator, so a stale named default never breaks the gate); scheme/signing notes; an ios-icon-gen skill (SF Symbols / Iconify → asset-catalog imageset). Tooling binary / scheme / destination overridable via userConfig.

stacks.name: Vue.js
stacks.description: Vue.js framework — per-version modules. The 2 module covers the Options API, Vue 2 reactivity caveats (Vue.set / array index), and @vue/test-utils 1.x + vue-jest SFC testing. Single-version pick.

stacks.name: Laravel Mix
stacks.description: Laravel Mix asset pipeline (webpack wrapper). The 5 module covers webpack.mix.js entry/output mapping, mix() versioning, the npm dev/watch/hot/prod scripts, and the legacy-OpenSSL prod-build flag on newer Node.

stacks.name: Python
stacks.description: Python language baseline + tooling. Modern Python (3.10+) idioms (type hints, dataclasses / pydantic, async-await discipline, logging-over-print, exception hierarchy), a post-edit ruff feedback hook (async, batched at Stop), and a pre-commit ruff-check + ruff-format + type-check (pyright|mypy) gate. Project root auto-detected by walking up to the nearest pyproject.toml, so monorepo backends (pyproject under backend/) work with zero config. Runner / binaries / type-checker overridable via userConfig (python_runner, ruff_bin, python_typechecker, python_project_roots). Framework-agnostic — pair with fastapi + pytest.

stacks.name: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy (async)
stacks.description: Async web-API guidance for FastAPI services: router + dependency-injection patterns, Pydantic schemas, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async session & transaction discipline, Alembic migration safety, CORS/auth/error handling. Skills + references only (no hooks) — the python module provides the lint/type-check hooks. Adds db / sec path triggers so database-reviewer and security-reviewer fire on model, migration, and auth edits.

stacks.name: pytest (async)
stacks.description: Python test strategy for async services: pytest + pytest-asyncio (asyncio_mode=auto), in-memory SQLite fixtures, httpx.AsyncClient + ASGITransport for FastAPI integration tests, unit/integration taxonomy, coverage-floor strategy, opt-in live markers. Skills + references only (no hooks). Provides test-framework conventions for the tdd-guide agent when active.

hook_profiles.description: Default profile. Sentinel-driven review reminders fire on relevant edits.
Standard + extra warnings (missing tools at SessionStart, docker container mismatch, etc.).

review_slots.notes: No built-in trigger — written by the library-author module's post-edit hook.
No built-in trigger — opt in via a module.yaml `migration:` triggers block (yii-1.1 ships one for protected/migrations/) or review_trigger_extra_paths 'mig:<prefix>'.

docker.notes: Container names are passed positionally to `--config docker_containers=<csv>`. The first entry is exported as DHPK_PHP_CONTAINER, the second as DHPK_MYSQL_CONTAINER, etc. Empty list disables the SessionStart docker check entirely.
